questions: tell me about the power grid
power grid er components ki ki?
answers: A power grid is the network of power plants, transmission lines, substations, and distribution systems that deliver electricity from producers to consumers.
Power grid holo electricity generation theke consumer porjonto puro network. Power plant theke high voltage line diye substation hoy, tarpor bari te ase.
The power grid consists of three main parts: generation (power plants), transmission (high-voltage lines), and distribution (local delivery to homes and businesses).
Power grids are massive interconnected systems that balance electricity supply and demand in real time. Grid operators ensure stable frequency and voltage across the entire network.
